claims.text: The capitalized cost to bring a single new drug to approval is about $2.6 billion in 2013 dollars, of which roughly $1.4B is out-of-pocket cash.
New drugs approved per inflation-adjusted billion dollars of R&D halved roughly every nine years from 1950, an ~80-fold collapse dubbed Eroom's Law.
Of every drug program entering Phase 1, only about 13.8% ever reaches approval, with Phase 2 the lowest-success transition at about 32%.
Recursion's Roche/Genentech deal was announced at up to $12 billion but had paid Recursion exactly $213 million as of November 2025.
Isomorphic Labs' 2024 Eli Lilly collaboration was announced at up to $1.7 billion but the upfront cash was $45 million.
Rentosertib improved forced vital capacity by +98.4 mL versus a -20.3 mL decline on placebo over 12 weeks in the randomized Phase 2a GENESIS-IPF trial.
AI-discovered molecules show an ~80-90% Phase 1 success rate but revert to ~40% in Phase 2, comparable to historic industry averages.
The AlphaFold Protein Structure Database holds over 200 million predicted structures and has been used by over 3 million researchers.
In Adaptyv Bio's open EGFR-binder competition only 53 of 378 expressed designs bound the target, a 14% wet-lab hit rate.
Eli Lilly deployed a wholly-owned NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with 1,016 Blackwell Ultra GPUs as a pharma AI factory.
The FDA's list of AI/ML-enabled medical devices has surpassed 1,000 marketing authorizations, of which roughly 76% are radiology.
DeepMind's AlphaMissense classified 89% of all 71 million possible human missense variants against a prior baseline where only about 0.1% had expert classification.
Tempus AI's precision-oncology data business reached about $1.3 billion in FY2025 revenue, up 83%, while still net-loss-making.
A RAND controlled red-team study found no statistically significant uplift from current LLMs for biological-attack planning.
Chinese biopharma out-licensing hit a record of about $135.7 billion across 157 deals in 2025, roughly 2.6x the 2024 total.
